content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach navigates significant bait without tripping any priority AoA anti-patterns. The client offers multiple invitations to validate the victim frame ('I was the available one,' disdain for ex partners as 'intense'), and the coach declines without colluding ('I hear you — you were showing up. And I'm not questioning that') while still gently turning toward her participation ('what if you were also a suspect?'). The coach avoids fixing-people (no partner-picking advice, no attachment-style diagnosis), avoids dont-evangelize-the-work (no AoA jargon deployed), and avoids wonder-as-strategy (questions are spaced, connective, and tracking the client's actual language rather than rapid-fire extraction). When the client self-criticizes ('who's uncomfortable being liked? That's insane'), the coach explicitly does NOT pile on or fight that voice — instead gently says 'That's not insane at all' and redirects to the felt specificity, avoiding both dont-fight-the-voice and overwhelm-of-creating-overwhelm. The coach also avoids loving-to-transform: there's no push for catharsis, just steady tracking of her own language back to her. The 'you choose this' point is held as invitation ('what if you were also a suspect') rather than accusation, exactly per the breakthrough condition.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 6, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens by checking the quality of her question rather than diagnosing — avoids view-as-technique."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What if you were also a suspect?' delivered as invitation/playfulness, not accusation — avoids the trap of pushing 'you choose this' as fact."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Explicitly declines to argue with her victim frame ('I'm not questioning that') while still redirecting toward her experience — avoids both colluding and fighting."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with body/feeling rather than escalating to interpretation; resists wonder-as-strategy."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "When client self-attacks ('who's uncomfortable being liked? That's insane'), coach says 'That's not insane at all' — avoids dont-fight-the-voice and avoids piling onto her self-criticism."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Simple 'yeah, you are' when she notices her own deflection — does not extract apology, does not shame, just stays with the material."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["looking-for-whats-wrong", "defensiveness-as-identity-signal", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "i-dont-buy-your-story"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["if-i-were-inherently-good"],
  "notes": "Coach declined the partner-picking bait, declined disdain-collusion, and held the 'you produce this' point as invitation. No anti-patterns tripped."
}
